facts.statement: The company is classified in the SEC submissions metadata under SIC description 'Services-Miscellaneous Amusement & Recreation' and is listed on the NYSE under ticker DIS.
Disney reported FY2025 revenues of $88.898 billion.
Disney reported FY2025 net income of $2.354 billion.
Disney reported FY2025 net cash provided by operating activities of $9.866 billion.
Disney reported stockholders' equity of $100.696 billion at FY2025.
Disney reported long-term debt of $38.970 billion and cash and equivalents of $14.182 billion at FY2025.
The 10-K and proxy materials identify Disney's operating businesses as Entertainment, Sports, and Experiences.
In fiscal 2025, the Entertainment DTC business generated $1.3 billion in operating income.
Management said Disney is integrating Disney+ and Hulu into a unified one-app experience.
Management said ESPN launched its full direct-to-consumer service and enhanced ESPN app, making ESPN's complete suite of networks and services available directly to consumers for the first time.
Management stated that over the past two years Disney delivered six franchise films that each generated more than $1 billion at the global box office.
For calendar 2025, Disney said it released three films that each exceeded $1 billion globally and that its total global box office receipts exceeded $6.5 billion.

Management said Disney has more expansion projects underway at its theme parks globally than ever before, including the largest expansion ever of Magic Kingdom at Walt Disney World, five additional cruise ships scheduled for launch beyond fiscal 2026, and a new theme park planned for development in Abu Dhabi.
Management said Disney increased its dividend by 50% to $1.50 and increased its target share repurchase by 100% in fiscal 2026.

The proxy statement says key risks include competitive pressure for content, talent and advertising; consumer acceptance of content, pricing and price increases; subscriber additions and churn; regulatory and legal developments; technological developments; labor markets and work stoppages; weather and natural disasters; and availability of content.

Disney disclosed active litigation including securities, fiduciary duty, antitrust, and other business-related claims, and said it cannot reasonably estimate the amount of any possible loss in certain cases.

facts.topic: capital allocation and debt risk management
facts.statement: Disney uses interest-rate swaps, foreign-exchange hedges, and cross-currency swaps to manage interest rate and foreign exchange risk; as of September 27, 2025, net foreign exchange cash flow hedges totaled $9.3 billion and pay-floating interest rate swaps had a notional amount of $10.6 billion.
Disney reported operating lease right-of-use assets of $3.170 billion and finance lease right-of-use assets of $221 million at September 27, 2025, with total lease liabilities of $3.397 billion.
Disney reported total lease cost of $1.348 billion in fiscal 2025 and cash paid for lease-related amounts of $974 million.
The proxy statement's non-GAAP reconciliation shows total segment operating income of $17.551 billion for fiscal 2025.
The proxy statement says the Board's Succession Planning Committee leads management succession oversight, and the Board currently expects to announce the next CEO in early 2026.
The proxy statement names Robert A. Iger as Chief Executive Officer and James P. Gorman as Chairman of the Board; it also identifies eleven director nominees for election at the 2026 annual meeting.

The proxy statement says the Compensation Committee made targeted enhancements in fiscal 2025 to strengthen the link between executive compensation and performance and align incentives with shareholder interests.
As of January 15, 2026, Disney disclosed The Vanguard Group as a holder of 8.5% of shares and BlackRock, Inc. as a holder of 6.9% of shares outstanding.
